heading: Injunctive relief sought by complainants
label: 41306 Injunctive relief sought by complainants
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)4130646 App.:1710(h)(2)., , .Pub. L. 98–237, § 11(h)(2)98 Stat. 81Mar. 20, 1984
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)4130646 App.:1710(h)(2)., , .Pub. L. 98–237, § 11(h)(2)98 Stat. 81Mar. 20, 1984

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: After filing a complaint with the Federal Maritime Commission under , the complainant may bring a civil action in a district court of the United States to enjoin conduct in violation of this part.section 41301 of this title
the Commission has brought a civil action against the defendant under ; orsection 41307(a) of this title
the defendant resides or transacts business, if the Commission has not brought such an action.

children.heading: .—Remedies by Court
children.label: c .—Remedies by Court
children.text: After notice to the defendant, and a showing that the standards for granting injunctive relief by courts of equity are met, the court may grant a temporary restraining order or preliminary injunction for a period not to exceed 10 days after the Commission has issued an order disposing of the complaint.

children.heading: .—Attorney Fees
children.label: d .—Attorney Fees
children.text: A defendant prevailing in a civil action under this section shall be allowed reasonable attorney fees to be assessed and collected as part of the costs of the action.
